title: The Way Forward – Fjord Review
content: World-class review of ballet and dance. It was my first visit to the Dance Commons, home of ODC/Dance nestled in San Francisco’s Mission District. Brenda Way, artistic director of ODC, looking summery on this Friday morning despite a bandaged ankle (nothing serious, routine dancer injury), met me in the foyer and took me on a tour. Kimi Okada and Brenda Way on tour with ODC to Seattle. Says Way pointing to the wooden beams and barn door of one ballet studio, “the architecture was inspired by Jacob's Pillow.” She cracks the door adjacent, and it's a different scene—the studio is jumping with Latin rhythms and people shimmying, sweating, smiling. “It was built by the dancers,” she says, “we had to have something which was kind of sixties.” A birthday party is currently underway. “It was a place to be intellectual in dance,” Way, who holds a PhD in Aesthetics and was recently awarded an honorary doctorate from Oberlin College, adds.

title: ODC/Dance review: Versatile, rewarding 'Sampler' - SFGATE
content: Kimi Okada's "Two If by Sea," scheduled for March's downtown season (and canceled, I gather, for lack of time), premiered to a roar of approval.
